ownerKind.description:
Optional: filter by owner. If not specified, returns submissions for the authenticated user/team.
Staff can specify any owner to view their submissions.

"user" or "team"

vacancyId.description: Empty => create new vacancy. Set => update existing.

isJoinable.description: If not provided by client, server may default to true.

includeVacancies.description: If false, server may omit vacancies for performance.

isFinal.title: true if automatically marked as final

contacts.title:
Contacts here are PUBLIC projection only (no per-contact visibility field).
- empty if hidden or include_contacts=false

skills.title:
Visibility applied on server:
- skills empty if hidden or include_skills=false

task.title: Optional fields, included based on request flags

filterGroups.description: OR between groups, AND inside group.

createdByUserId.title:
User who actually created/uploaded this submission
(for team submissions, this is the team member who uploaded)

isFinal.title: Whether this is the final submission selected by the owner

ownerKind.title: Owner of the submission (user or team)

isJoinable.description: When true, participants can create JoinRequests to this team.

assignedVacancyId.description: Empty if the member is not assigned to any vacancy (e.g. team creator/captain).

desiredRoleIds.description: Client may use a special value like "any" to represent "ANY".

desiredSkillIds.description: Client may use a special value like "any" to represent "ANY".

post.description: Checks whether an access token is still active and returns its claims (user ID, expiry).
Authenticates the user and returns a pair of JWT tokens (access + refresh).
Invalidates the provided refresh token so it can no longer be used.
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token and a rotated refresh token.
Creates a new user and returns a pair of JWT tokens (access + refresh).
Creates a new hackathon in DRAFT state. The caller becomes the OWNER. If `validationErrors` is non-empty the hackathon was still created but contains invalid data — fix the fields before publishing.
Returns a paginated list of hackathons. Supports filtering and ordering via the `query` field.

get.description: Fetches a single hackathon. Use the `include_*` flags to load optional sections (description, links, limits, task, result).

put.description: Updates hackathon fields. If `validationErrors` is non-empty the update was rejected — fix the listed fields and retry.

get.description: Returns all announcements for the hackathon in reverse-chronological order.

post.description: Creates a new announcement for the hackathon. Visible to all participants immediately. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an announcement. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.

put.description: Updates the title and body of an existing announcement. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.

get.description: Returns a ranked list of participants that match the team's vacancy requirements. Available during REGISTRATION stage.
get.summary: Get candidate recommendations for team captain

get.description: Returns a ranked list of teams that match the participant's skills and desired roles. Available during REGISTRATION and RUNNING stages.

Returns the final submission for a participant or team. Owner can view their own final submission. Staff (owners, organizers, mentors, judges) can view any final submission. Available from RUNNING stage onwards.

post.description: Returns a paginated list of participants. Filterable by status and wished roles. Requires the caller to be staff or a registered participant.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's participation record for the given hackathon.

put.description: Updates wished team roles and motivation text for the authenticated user's participation.

post.description: Switches the authenticated user between individual and looking-for-team participation modes.
Withdraws the authenticated user from the hackathon. The participation record is soft-deleted.
Registers the authenticated user for a hackathon. Depending on `desiredStatus`, the user joins as an individual (`PART_INDIVIDUAL`) or applies to join a team (`PART_LOOKING_FOR_TEAM`).

get.description: Returns another user's participation record. Requires the caller to be staff or themselves a registered participant of the hackathon.

post.description: Transitions the hackathon from DRAFT to PUBLISHED, making it visible to participants. The hackathon must pass validation first.

get.description: Returns the published result announcement (Markdown). Returns empty if the result has not been published yet.

put.description: Saves a draft of the result announcement. Does NOT publish it. If `validationErrors` is non-empty the update was rejected.

post.description: Makes the result draft visible to all participants. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.

get.description: Returns all staff members and their roles for the given hackathon. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.

post.description: Creates a staff invitation for the target user. They can accept or reject it from their invitations list. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.
Cancels a pending invitation so the target user can no longer accept it. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role.
Removes a specific role from a staff member. Requires OWNER role.
Allows a staff member to relinquish one of their own roles. An OWNER cannot remove their own OWNER role if they are the sole owner.
post.summary: Voluntarily give up a staff role

post.description: Creates a new submission for the hackathon. Automatically marks it as final if no other final submission exists. Available only during RUNNING stage.

Returns submissions for the hackathon. If owner_kind and owner_id are specified, returns submissions for that owner. If not specified, returns submissions for the authenticated user/team. Staff (owners, organizers, mentors, judges) can list submissions for any participant/team. Available from RUNNING stage onwards.

get.description: Returns a submission by ID. Owner can view their own submissions. Staff (owners, organizers, mentors, judges) can view any submission. Available from RUNNING stage onwards.

put.description: Updates the description of a submission. Only the creator can update. Available only during RUNNING stage.

post.description: Initiates a file upload and returns a pre-signed PUT URL. Validates file type and size limits. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Marks the file upload as completed after successful upload to S3. Available only during RUNNING stage.

get.description: Returns a pre-signed GET URL for downloading a submission file. Owner can download their own files. Staff (owners, organizers, mentors, judges) can download any submission files. Available from RUNNING stage onwards.

post.description: Marks a submission as final. Unmarks any previously selected final submission. Only team captain (for team submissions) or individual participant can perform this. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Participant sends a message to support. Creates a ticket if needed. Available only during RUNNING stage.

get.description: Returns all messages from all tickets (open and closed) for the authenticated participant. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Returns a JWT token for connecting to Centrifugo WebSocket for real-time updates.

post.description: Returns all tickets in the hackathon. Requires OWNER or ORGANIZER role. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Returns tickets assigned to the authenticated mentor. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Allows an organizer to assign an unassigned ticket to a specific mentor. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Allows a mentor to claim an unassigned ticket. Ticket must be OPEN and unassigned. Available only during RUNNING stage.
Mentor or organizer closes a ticket. Available only during RUNNING stage.

get.description: Returns messages for a specific ticket. Only mentors and organizers can access. Participants should use GetMyChatMessages instead. Available only during RUNNING stage.

post.description: Mentor replies in an assigned ticket. Available only during RUNNING stage.

get.description: Returns the task description (Markdown) for the hackathon. Requires the hackathon to be published or the caller to be staff.

put.description: Replaces the task description. Requires OWNER or MANAGER role. If `validationErrors` is non-empty the update was rejected.

post.description: Creates a team in the hackathon and makes the authenticated user its captain. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage when team participation is enabled by the hackathon policy.
Returns a paginated list of teams for the hackathon. Requires the caller to be staff (OWNER/ORGANIZER/MENTOR) or a registered participant. Available starting from the REGISTRATION stage.

delete.description: Deletes the team. Only the team captain can perform this action, and only if they are the sole member. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.

get.description: Returns a team and, optionally, its vacancies. Requires the caller to be staff (OWNER/ORGANIZER/MENTOR) or a registered participant. Available starting from the REGISTRATION stage.

put.description: Updates team fields (name, description, is_joinable). Only the team captain can perform this action. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.

post.description: Requests to join a team for a specific vacancy. The authenticated user must be a registered participant. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Returns join requests received by the team (incoming requests). Only the team captain can access this list. Available starting from the REGISTRATION stage.
post.summary: List join requests for a team

post.description: Accepts a pending join request. The requester joins the team as a member, a vacancy slot is consumed, and competing pending invitations/requests in the same hackathon are canceled. Only the team captain can perform this action. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Rejects a pending join request. The request is marked as rejected and cannot be accepted later. Only the team captain can perform this action. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.

get.description: Returns the team roster. Requires the caller to be staff (OWNER/ORGANIZER/MENTOR) or a registered participant. Available starting from the REGISTRATION stage.

post.description: Removes the authenticated user from the team. Only non-captain members can leave. This action is allowed outside REGISTRATION (from REGISTRATION through FINISHED), provided team participation is enabled by the hackathon policy.
Removes a member from the team. Only the team captain can perform this action. Kicking the captain is forbidden. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Invites a user to join the team for a specific vacancy. Only the team captain can perform this action. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Returns invitations created by the team (outgoing invitations). Only the team captain can access this list. Available starting from the REGISTRATION stage.
post.summary: List team invitations for a team

post.description: Cancels a pending team invitation so the target user can no longer accept it. Only the team captain can perform this action. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Transfers captainship to another team member. Only the current captain can perform this action. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Creates or updates a vacancy for the team. Only the team captain can perform this action. Slots constraints and team size limits are enforced. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.

get.description: Checks whether a hackathon is ready to be published. Returns all blocking validation errors; an empty list means it is safe to publish.

post.description: Returns a paginated list of skills from the global catalog. Use when displaying a skill picker.

get.description: Returns all available team roles that a participant can indicate they are looking for (e.g. Frontend, Backend, Designer).

post.description: Fetches multiple users' public profiles in a single request. Useful for enriching participant lists.
Returns a paginated list of users. Supports text search and ordering via the `query` field.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's full profile including skills, contacts, and visibility settings.

put.description: Updates basic profile fields (first name, last name, avatar URL, timezone) for the authenticated user.
Replaces the authenticated user's contact list entirely (replace-all semantics). Also updates contacts visibility.

post.description: Returns join requests created by the authenticated user.
Cancels a pending join request. Only the requester can cancel their own request. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.

put.description: Replaces the authenticated user's skill set entirely (replace-all semantics). Also updates skills visibility.

post.description: Returns pending staff invitations addressed to the authenticated user.
Accepts a pending invitation. The authenticated user is added to the hackathon staff with the requested role.
Rejects a pending invitation. The invitation is marked as rejected and cannot be accepted later.
Returns team invitations addressed to the authenticated user.

Accepts a pending team invitation. The authenticated user joins the team as a member, a vacancy slot is consumed, and competing pending invitations/requests in the same hackathon are canceled. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.
Rejects a pending team invitation. The invitation is marked as rejected and cannot be accepted later. Allowed only during the REGISTRATION stage.

get.description: Returns a user's public profile. Skills and contacts are included only when the respective `include_*` flag is set AND the user has not hidden them.
